Homework: The Meat of It

Task 2 - create string called Mary Had A Little Lamb in one code block, then split the string it so each word has its own line. Then add a variable called Nletters that counts the letters in each word.

# Create a string of Mary Had a Little Lamb
mary <- "Mary had a little lamb whose fleece was white as snow and everywhere that Mary went that lamb was sure it go it followed her to school one day which was against the rules it made the children laugh and play to see that lamb at school"

str(mary)
##  chr "Mary had a little lamb whose fleece was white as snow and everywhere that Mary went that lamb was sure it go it"| __truncated__
# Split this string based on spaces (each word separated by a space is a
# separate string)
split_mary <- strsplit(mary, split = " ")
print(split_mary)
## [[1]]
##  [1] "Mary"       "had"        "a"          "little"     "lamb"      
##  [6] "whose"      "fleece"     "was"        "white"      "as"        
## [11] "snow"       "and"        "everywhere" "that"       "Mary"      
## [16] "went"       "that"       "lamb"       "was"        "sure"      
## [21] "it"         "go"         "it"         "followed"   "her"       
## [26] "to"         "school"     "one"        "day"        "which"     
## [31] "was"        "against"    "the"        "rules"      "it"        
## [36] "made"       "the"        "children"   "laugh"      "and"       
## [41] "play"       "to"         "see"        "that"       "lamb"      
## [46] "at"         "school"
# Convert this list to dataframe
mary_df <- as.data.frame(split_mary)

# Change column name from the entire string to 'word'
colnames(mary_df) <- c("word")

# Add column for Nletters for each word
mary_df <- mary_df %>%
    mutate(Nchar = nchar(mary_df$word))

mary_df
word Nchar
Mary 4
had 3
a 1
little 6
lamb 4
whose 5
fleece 6
was 3
white 5
as 2
snow 4
and 3

that 4
